title: No Option to Cancel a Wrong Transaction – Extremely Frustrating Experience
text:
I am extremely disappointed with this app and the overall experience. I made a transaction, but due to a small mistake while entering the owner’s mobile number, the transaction got stuck in the verification process. The worst part is that there is no option to cancel the transaction or edit the incorrect details.

Instead of allowing users to simply cancel the transaction and create a new one with the correct information, the only solution provided was to wait for the transaction to fail and then wait 9–10 business days for the refund. This is completely unreasonable. A simple mistake should not force users to wait for weeks just to get their own money back.

Because of this, I am unable to complete the verification or create a new transaction immediately. The app lacks basic features like Cancel Transaction, Edit Details, or Retry Verification, which are essential for any platform handling important transactions.

The customer support experience was also disappointing. Rather than offering a practical solution, I was simply told to wait for the refund. This wastes users’ time, delays important work, and creates unnecessary frustration.

The developers seriously need to improve the user experience by allowing users to cancel pending transactions, correct mistakes, and process refunds much faster. Until these issues are fixed, I cannot recommend this app to anyone.

Very poor user experience. I expected much better from an app that handles important transactions.

title: I had a very disappointing experience with PhonePe. A person sold me a POS machine for ₹11,000 by cl
text:
I had a very disappointing experience with PhonePe. A person sold me a POS machine for ₹11,000 by claiming it was a lifetime machine. After taking the payment, he stopped answering my calls and messages. Later, a PhonePe representative informed me that the machine was not a lifetime machine, had been issued in someone else’s name, and was worth much less than what I paid.

When I contacted PhonePe through its official support, I was asked to provide the employee’s ID card. As a customer, how can I have an employee’s ID card? If that person was part of PhonePe’s sales network, the company should verify its own records instead of asking customers for internal documents.

I lost ₹11,000 and did not receive the support I expected. I hope PhonePe investigates such complaints seriously and ensures that no other customer faces a similar experience.
